title: Huang's Conjecture
statement_text: The 4-precoloring extension problem can be solved in polynomial time for the class of $P_6$-free graphs.
context_text: Huang conjectured that 4-coloring (and 4-precoloring extension) is polynomial-time solvable for $P_6$-free graphs. This was the last unresolved case in the complexity classification of the 4-coloring problem for graphs with a connected forbidden induced subgraph, since all cases with $k\geq 5$ or $t\leq 5$ or $t\geq 7$ were already settled. Resolving this conjecture also immediately implies that the 4-coloring problem for $P_6$-free graphs is solvable in polynomial time.
paper_contribution: This paper (the second in a two-paper series) gives a polynomial-time algorithm that converts any 4-precoloring of a $P_6$-free graph into a polynomial-size collection of excellent starred precolorings; combined with the companion paper's algorithm for excellent precolorings, the two papers together prove Huang's conjecture in full.
paper_title: Four-coloring $P_6$-free graphs. II. Finding an excellent precoloring
